metadata.name: mquery
metadata.summary: Blazingly fast Yara queries for malware analysts

classes.name: models.queuedfile.QueuedFile
documentation: Represents a file that is waiting to be indexed.

classes.name: models.agentgroup.AgentGroup
documentation:
Agent group is a group of processes working on a single
file group, with a shared storage, and a single backing ursadb.

classes.name: models.match.Match
documentation: Represents a file matched to a job, along with a related metadata.

classes.name: models.job.JobView

class_variables.name: models.job.JobView.status
documentation: type: ignore
Public fields of mquery jobs.

classes.name: models.job.Job
documentation: Job object in the database. Internal ID is an implementation detail.

classes.name: models.jobagent.JobAgent
documentation: Information about job relating to a specific agent group.

methods.name: metadata.MetadataPlugin.identify
documentation:
Returns file unique identifier based on matched path.

Intended to be overridden by plugin.

methods.name: metadata.MetadataPlugin.run
documentation:
Extracts metadata and updates cache. This method can only be run if
the plugin sets `is_extractor` to True.

```
param matched_fname

Filename of the processed file

param current_meta

Metadata that will be updated

return

New metadata
```

methods.name: metadata.MetadataPlugin.filter
documentation:
Checks if the file is a good candidate for further processing,
and fix the file path if necessary.
\:param matched_fname: Original file path coming from ursadb
\:param file_path: Current path to the file contents
\:return: New path to a file (may be the same path). None if the file
should be discarded.

methods.name: metadata.MetadataPlugin.cleanup
documentation:
Optionally, clean up after the plugin, for example remove any
temporary files. Called after processing a single batch of files.

methods.name: metadata.MetadataPlugin.extract
documentation:
Extracts metadata for matched path.

Intended to be overridden by plugin, if is_extractor is True.

```
param identifier

File identifier returned by overridable
 method

param matched_fname

Matched file path

param current_meta

Metadata extracted so far by dependencies

return

Metadata object. If you can't extract metadata for current file,
return empty dict.
```

functions.name: app.with_plugins
documentation: Cleans up plugins after processing.

functions.name: app.get_user_roles
documentation:
Get all roles assigned to user, taking into account the
system configuration (like default configured roles)

functions.name: app.expand_role
documentation:
Some roles imply other roles or permissions. For example, admin role
also gives permissions for all user permissions.

functions.name: app.config_list
documentation:
Returns the current database configuration.

This endpoint is not stable and may be subject to change in the future.
Admin-only routes (when user permissions are configured).
Non-admins can't use them, and shouldn't see them in the UI.

functions.name: app.config_edit
documentation:
Change a given configuration key to a specified value.

This endpoint is not stable and may be subject to change in the future.

functions.name: app.backend_status
documentation:
Gets the current status of backend services, and returns it. Intended to
be used by the webpage.

This endpoint is not stable and may be subject to change in the future.

functions.name: app.backend_status_datasets
documentation:
Returns a combined list of datasets from all agents.

Caveat: In case of collision of dataset ids when there are multiple agents,
this API will only return one dataset per colliding ID. Collision is
extremally unlikely though and it shouldn't be a problem in the real world.

This endpoint is not stable and may be subject to change in the future.

functions.name: app.download
documentation:
Sends a file from given `file_path`. This path should come from
results of one of the previous searches.

This endpoint needs `job_id` that found the specified file, and `ordinal`
(index of the file in that job), to ensure that user can't download
arbitrary files (for example "/etc/passwd").
Standard authenticated routes (when user permissions are configured).
Accessible for every logged in user (permission: "reader")

functions.name: app.download_hashes
documentation: Returns a list of job matches as a sha256 strings joined with newlines.

functions.name: app.zip_files
documentation:
Adds all the samples to a zip archive (replacing original filename
with sha256) and returns it as a stream of bytes.

functions.name: app.query
documentation:
Starts a new search. Response will contain a new job ID that can be used
to check the job status and download matched files.

functions.name: app.matches
documentation:
Returns a list of matched files, along with metadata tags and other
useful information. Results from this query can be used to download files
using the `/download` endpoint.

functions.name: app.job_info
documentation:
Returns a metadata for a single job. May be useful for monitoring
a job progress.

functions.name: app.job_cancel
documentation: Cancels the job with a provided `job_id`.

functions.name: app.job_statuses
documentation:
Returns statuses of all the jobs in the system. May take some time (> 1s)
when there are a lot of them.

functions.name: app.server
documentation: Permissionless routes.
1. Static routes are always publicly accessible without authorisation.
2. /api/server is a special route always accessible for everyone.

methods.name: lib.ursadb.PopResult.iterator_empty
documentation: Is it safe to remove the iterator after this operation?

methods.name: lib.ursadb.PopResult.__str__
documentation: Pretty-print iterator showing all important information.

class_variables.name: schema.QueryRequestSchema.reference
documentation: arbitrary data specified by the user

classes.name: plugins.s3_plugin.S3Plugin
documentation:
Can be used to download files from minio prior to running yara.
Names of the files in configured bucket must be equal to basenames
(filenames without paths) of matched files.

classes.name: plugins.blacklist.RegexBlacklistPlugin
documentation:
Can be used to ignore files with filenames matching a certain
pattern. For example, to ignore all pcap files, set blacklist_pattern
to "[.]pcap$".

functions.name: plugins.parse_plugin_list
documentation:
Parses and validates a plugin list into a list of non-empty components
divided by `,`.

```pycon
>>> parse_plugin_list("plugins.Test:A, plugins.Other:A")
["plugins.Test:A", "plugins.Other:A"]
```

```pycon
>>> parse_plugin_list("")
[]
```

```
param plugins

String with a list of comma separated plugins

return

List of plugins, with no unnecessary spaces.
```

methods.name: plugins.PluginManager.filter
documentation:
Runs all available filter plugins on the provided file.
Returns new file path, or None. User should call cleanup() later.

methods.name: plugins.PluginManager.cleanup
documentation:
Clean up all plugin state. Worth stressing that plugins are *not* thread
safe, and running filter() and cleanup() from different threads will cause
problems. Running a plugin multiple times before the cleanup should be ok.

modules.name: plugins.example_typed_config_plugin
documentation:
Plugin that serves as an example how to use existing typed-config
machinery to configure your own plugins.

classes.name: plugins.example_typed_config_plugin.ExamplePluginConfig
documentation: Plugin configuration.

classes.name: plugins.example_typed_config_plugin.ExamplePluginWithTypedConfig
documentation:
This plugin serves as an example how to use typed-config and
mquery config file to configure your own plugins. It's equivalent
to ExamplePlugin in all except the configuration method.
You will need to add this to your config file (or use env vars):
[plugin.example]
tag=kot
tag_url=http://google.com

classes.name: plugins.example_plugin.ExampleTagPlugin
documentation:
This plugin is a minimal (almost) example of extractor plugin.
It will tag every processed file with configured tag and URL.

classes.name: plugins.archive.GzipPlugin
documentation:
Can be used to automatically extract gzip contents before running
Yara on them. This plugin will look for all files that end with .gz,
and add extract them to disk before further processing.

classes.name: lib.yaraparse.UrsaExpression
documentation:
Represents a single Ursadb SELECT expression body. In the future
this may be represented as an expression tree, for example.

Examples of valid expressions are:

"xyz"
"xyz" & "www"
({112233} | "xxx") & "hmm"

Some UrsaExpressions are "degenerate" expressions - this means that
they have no useful checks and UrsaDb can't do anything with them.
Some examples are "", ("" | "abc") or min 2 of ("", "", "abc").

methods.name: lib.yaraparse.RuleParseEngine.gt_expr
documentation: From `#a > 3` we can deduce, that #a must occur at least once.

methods.name: lib.yaraparse.RuleParseEngine.lt_expr
documentation: From `3 < #a` we can deduce, that #a must occur at least once.

methods.name: lib.yaraparse.RuleParseEngine.ge_expr
documentation: From `#a >= y` we can deduce, that #a must occur at least once if y>0.

methods.name: lib.yaraparse.RuleParseEngine.le_expr
documentation: From `y <= #a` we can deduce, that #a must occur at least once if y>0.

methods.name: lib.yaraparse.RuleParseEngine.eq_expr
documentation: From `x == literal` and literal > 0 we can deduce that x must occur.

modules.name: daemon
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: daemon.main
documentation:
Spawns a new agent process. Use argv if you want to use a different
group_id (it's `default` by default).

classes.name: db.UserRole

class_variables.name: db.UserRole.nobody
documentation: no permissions granted

class_variables.name: db.UserRole.user
documentation: can run yara queries and read the state

class_variables.name: db.UserRole.admin
documentation: can manage the system (and do everything else)
See docs/users.md for documentation on the permission model.
Enum values are meaningless and may change. Make sure to not store them
anywhere (for storing/transfer use role names instead).

methods.name: db.Database.__schedule
documentation: Schedules the task to agent group `agent` using rq.

methods.name: db.Database.cancel_job
documentation: Sets the job status to cancelled, with optional error message.

methods.name: db.Database.fail_job
documentation: Sets the job status to cancelled with provided error message.

methods.name: db.Database.__get_job
documentation: Internal helper to get a job from the database.

methods.name: db.Database.get_job
documentation: Retrieves a job from the database.

methods.name: db.Database.get_valid_jobs
documentation: Retrieves valid (accessible) jobs from the database.

methods.name: db.Database.remove_query
documentation: Delete the job, linked match and job agent from the database.

methods.name: db.Database.job_contains
documentation: Make sure that the file path is in the job results.

methods.name: db.Database.job_start_work
documentation:
Updates the number of files being processed right now.
\:param job: ID of the job being updated.
\:param in_progress: Number of files in the current work unit.

methods.name: db.Database.agent_finish_job
documentation:
Decrements the number of active agents in the given job. If there
are no more agents, job status is changed to done.

methods.name: db.Database.init_jobagent
documentation:
Creates a new JobAgent object.
If tasks==0 then finishes job immediately.

methods.name: db.Database.agent_add_tasks_in_progress
documentation:
Increments (or decrements, for negative values) the number of tasks
that are in progress for agent. The number of tasks in progress should
always stay positive for jobs in status inprogress. This function will
automatically call agent_finish_job if the agent has no more tasks left.

methods.name: db.Database.job_update_work
documentation:
Updates progress for the job. This will increment numbers processed,
inprogress, errored and matched files.
Returns the number of processed files after the operation.

methods.name: db.Database.init_job_datasets
documentation: Sets total_datasets and datasets_left, and status to processing.

methods.name: db.Database.dataset_query_done
documentation: Decrements the number of datasets left by one.

methods.name: db.Database.create_search_task
documentation: Creates a new job object in the db, and schedules daemon tasks.

methods.name: db.Database.update_job_files
documentation: Add total_files to the specified job, and return a new total.

methods.name: db.Database.register_active_agent
documentation:
Update or create a Agent information row in the database.
Returns the new or existing agent ID.

methods.name: db.Database.get_core_config
documentation: Gets a list of configuration fields for the mquery core.

functions.name: tasks.job_context
documentation: Small error-handling context manager. Fails the job on exception.

functions.name: tasks.make_agent
documentation: Creates a new agent using the default settings from config.

functions.name: tasks.ursadb_command
documentation: Executes a raw ursadb command using this backend.

functions.name: tasks.start_search
documentation:
Initialises a search task - checks available datasets and schedules smaller
units of work.

functions.name: tasks.__get_batch_sizes
documentation:
Returns a list of integers that sums to file_count. The idea is to split
the work between all workers into units that are not too small and not
too big. Currently just creates equally sized batches.

functions.name: tasks.query_ursadb
documentation: Queries ursadb and creates yara scans tasks with file batches.

functions.name: tasks.run_yara_batch
documentation: Actually scans files, and updates a database with the results.

functions.name: tasks.read_bytes_with_context
documentation: Return `matched_length` bytes from `offset`, along with `byte_range` bytes before and after the match.

methods.name: tasks.Agent.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new agent instance. Every agents belongs to some group
(identified by the group_id). There may be multiple agent workers in a
single group, but they must all work on the same ursadb instance.
Reads connection parameters and plugins from the global config.

methods.name: tasks.Agent.register
documentation:
Register the agent in the database. Should happen when starting
the worker process.

methods.name: tasks.Agent.get_datasets
documentation: Returns a list of dataset IDs, or throws an exception on error.

methods.name: tasks.Agent.update_metadata
documentation:
Saves matches to the database, and runs appropriate metadata
plugins.

methods.name: tasks.Agent.add_tasks_in_progress
documentation: See documentation of db.agent_add_tasks_in_progress.
